title: Fix Terminal Input after Biometric Auth
description: Fix terminal keyboard input bug occurring after Windows Hello authentication

steps.title: Add terminal state recovery function
steps.description: Create a function in `src/terminal.rs` called `restore_terminal_state` that toggles raw mode on and off using `crossterm::terminal::enable_raw_mode` and `disable_raw_mode` to force the terminal to rebuild its state.
steps.summary: Added `restore_terminal_state` function to `src/terminal.rs` to toggle raw mode on and off, resetting terminal console modes.

steps.title: Apply terminal recovery post-authentication
steps.description: Call `terminal::restore_terminal_state()` in `src/main.rs` immediately after a successful `auth::verify()` check.
steps.expectedOutcome: The application will successfully restore terminal input modes after the OS-level Windows Hello dialog closes, preventing the `inquire` prompts from misinterpreting keyboard input.
steps.summary: Called `terminal::restore_terminal_state()` in `src/main.rs` right after a successful `auth::verify()` call. This ensures the terminal mode is fully reset before `inquire` begins presenting prompts.

context:
The user reported broken terminal input after authenticating with Windows Hello. The likely culprit is the Windows Hello dialog silently mutating the Windows Console Modes, breaking `crossterm`'s assumptions when `inquire` subsequently attempts to read keyboard input.

We have written our findings to `.tide/research.md`.
